title: SDAP Handshake Accept
description: The second message in the SDAP three-way handshake (Layer 2). Sent by the target agent in response to a valid handshake-init message. The responder has resolved the initiator's DID, verified the init message signature, and confirms its own identity by signing this response. Both agents can now derive the shared session key from the two ephemeral X25519 keys using ECDH + HKDF-SHA256.

type.description: Message type discriminator. MUST be exactly 'sdap/handshake/accept'.

sessionId.description: A UUID v4 assigned by the responder to identify this session. All subsequent session messages (envelopes, audit entries) MUST reference this sessionId. The initiator MUST store this value and use it in the handshake-confirm message.

responderDID.description: The DID of the agent responding to the handshake. MUST match the targetDID from the corresponding handshake-init message. The initiator MUST verify this matches the DID it intended to contact.

initiatorDID.description: The DID of the initiating agent, echoed from the handshake-init message. Allows stateless correlation of messages.

nonce.description: A new cryptographically random nonce generated by the responder, base64url-encoded (no padding), exactly 32 bytes (43 characters). The initiator MUST echo this nonce in the handshake-confirm message to prove it received this accept message.

initiatorNonce.description: The nonce from the handshake-init message, echoed back verbatim. Proves that this accept message is a response to that specific init and not a replay.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this accept message was generated. Initiators MUST reject accept messages with timestamps more than 300 seconds from the init message timestamp.

ephemeralKey.description: The responder's ephemeral X25519 public key in JWK format, generated fresh for this handshake. Combined with the initiator's ephemeral key via ECDH to produce the shared secret, from which the session key is derived via HKDF-SHA256 with the two nonces as additional input.

grantedScopes.description: The subset of requestedScopes from the handshake-init that the responder is granting for this session. MUST be a subset of the initiator's requestedScopes — the responder cannot grant scopes the initiator did not request. An empty array means no scopes are granted (the session is established but the initiator has no data access permissions).

sessionExpiry.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp indicating when this session will expire. After this time, the session key MUST be considered invalid and a new handshake MUST be performed. Responders SHOULD set this to no more than 3600 seconds (1 hour) for standard security level and 900 seconds (15 minutes) for high/critical.

signature.description: A compact JWS signature over the JCS-canonicalized message body (excluding the 'signature' field), using the responder's Ed25519 authentication key. The JWS header 'kid' parameter MUST reference the key's DID URL in the responder's DID Document.

supportedLayers.description: The SDAP layers (1–5) supported by the responder. Allows the initiator to know which capabilities are available before sending delegation tokens or expecting audit entries.

negotiatedSecurityLevel.description: The security level that was negotiated for this session, taking into account the requiredSecurityLevel from the init message and the responder's capabilities. Will be equal to or higher than the initiator's requiredSecurityLevel.

x25519PublicKeyJWK.description: An X25519 public key in JSON Web Key format (RFC 7517 + RFC 8037).
